changes_made.description: Baseline: original Chinese-language verbose skill (~340 lines)
Rewrote in English, added task-specific guidance for kebab-case conversion (preserve dots in versions) and directory analysis (count hidden files, extension=last dot). Reduced to ~60 lines.
Added explicit reference table of example renames. Emphasized writing rename_log.json AFTER renames. Added step-by-step bash commands for directory analysis.
Noted model was using unreliable bash one-liners. Switched to providing shell commands approach with explicit examples. Results regressed due to model stochasticity.
Switched to providing complete Python scripts for both tasks with directive to write and execute them. Added IMPORTANT notice to use scripts not manual commands. fs-batch-rename hit 100/100.

key_findings: qwen3-30b is highly stochastic - same skill can produce different scores across runs
Chinese-language skill was ineffective for this model despite Qwen's multilingual training
Providing complete executable Python scripts is more reliable than bash commands or procedural instructions
The dot-in-version-number edge case (v3.1) is the hardest part of kebab-case conversion
File counting errors (missing hidden files, wrong extension grouping) persist even with explicit instructions
fs-search counts criterion is the most fragile - model sometimes ignores the script and does manual counting
